meta.decision_rationale: Narrative system translates brand DNA and refusals into language that resonates with different community audiences (patrons, city council, donors, staff).
tradeoffs: Multiple narratives require consistent underlying values
Different audiences need different language, same substance
rejected_alternatives: Single narrative (doesn't speak to diverse stakeholders)

payload.core_narrative: We are a community library reimagined as a democratic institution. We protect intellectual freedom, guarantee equitable access, and govern ourselves through community voice—not corporate algorithms or political control.

audience_narratives.narrative: This is YOUR library. We exist to serve your curiosity, your research, your joy in reading—not to profit from you or control what you think. Your voice shapes our decisions.
We are a cost-effective civic institution that builds social cohesion, supports literacy, and strengthens democratic participation. We're fiscally responsible stewards of public investment.
Invest in a library that proves democracy can work at the local level. We're building a model of community governance that protects intellectual freedom while building trust.
You are not order-takers. You are partners in a governance experiment that respects professional expertise, community input, and your own intellectual growth.

key_messages: Intellectual freedom isn't luxury—it's essential infrastructure for democracy
Community-governed institutions can be more responsive than corporate or purely top-down models
Equity and efficiency aren't opposed; shared decision-making builds sustainable community support
